error_signature: date output differs between bash and n8n SQL exec
title: Date output inconsistency in Postgres SQL execution node
symptom: The date returned by the Postgres SQL execution node in n8n is different from the date returned in the bash environment. n8n shows the date in UTC instead of the expected local timezone.
applies_when: Using Postgres SQL execution node in n8n
not_this_if: Not using the Postgres SQL execution node
root_cause: The discrepancy arises because n8n's SQL execution node returns date-like values in UTC format, while the bash environment reflects the local timezone settings. This can lead to confusion when interpreting date outputs.
fix_steps: Modify your SQL query to return dates as text instead of date/timestamp types.
Use the format 'SELECT (NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Istanbul')::text AS local_date;' to ensure the output is in the desired format.
agent_instruction: Change your SQL queries to return dates as text to avoid timezone issues.
